citation: Martin AL, Anadon CM, Biswas S, et al. (2022). "Olfactory Receptor OR2H1 is an effective target for CAR T cells in human epithelial tumors." Mol Cancer Ther 21(7):1184-1194. doi:10.1158/1535-7163.MCT-21-0872
claim: OR2H1 is selectively expressed on multiple solid epithelial tumors (ovarian, non-small-cell lung, cholangiocarcinoma) with no detectable expression in 58 healthy human tissues (except testis), making it an effective CAR-T target: OR2H1-targeted CAR-T cells achieve ~90% specific cytotoxic killing in vitro and abrogate/delay tumor growth in vivo (NSG mouse xenografts).
mechanism: Phage-display-derived scFv against the OR2H1 extracellular domain, fused via glycine/serine linker to CD8-alpha hinge/transmembrane domains and 4-1BB/CD3zeta intracellular signaling domains (2nd-generation CAR). CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of OR2H1 in tumor cells abolished CAR-T cytotoxicity, confirming target specificity. OR2H1 knockout also reduced glucose uptake (2-NBDG assay), suggesting a role in tumor cell metabolism beyond being a passive surface marker.
model_system: H2009 (NSCLC) and OVCAR3 (ovarian) cell lines in vitro; NSG mouse xenografts in vivo (H2009 flank tumors, OVCAR3 tumors)
verification_notes: Pulled and read full text via PMC (PMC9256805). Exact figures verified: OR2H1 expression in 13% of lung carcinomas (8/60), 20% of ovarian carcinomas (8/40), 59% of cholangiocarcinomas (43/73); 0% in 58 healthy tissues except testis. Mouse experiments: n=5/group (H2009), n=4-5/group (OVCAR3), each pooled from 2 independent replicates; in vitro assays repeated >=2x. Direction is labeled 'tumor-promoting' here in the loose sense of 'a marker of aggressive/malignant epithelial phenotype useful as a therapeutic target' -- this paper does not claim OR2H1 activation itself drives tumor growth (unlike the OR51E2 literature), it establishes OR2H1 as a surface antigen for immunotherapy targeting. Cross-checked against the friend's (Bansal/Gudisay) Literature_Review.docx, which independently confirms the qRT-PCR/Western blot/CRISPR/glucose-metabolism/RNAscope methodology in more detail than the abstract alone.
raw_excerpt_or_link: doi:10.1158/1535-7163.MCT-21-0872; corroborating summary in data/intake/or51e2/Literature Review.docx (this review is not receptor-scoped despite its filename, and covers OR2H1 in depth)

citation: GDC API cnvs endpoint, gene ENSG00000204688, live pull
claim: OR2H1 CNV gain/amplification observed across 45+ TCGA projects, 2929 unique cases total. Notable per the Martin et al. 2022 CAR-T paper's cancer types: TCGA-OV (ovarian) 253 cases, TCGA-LUAD 178 cases, TCGA-LUSC 116 cases, TCGA-CHOL (cholangiocarcinoma) 7 cases -- CHOL is a small cohort (~36 cases total in TCGA), so 7 is a comparatively high frequency, consistent with the paper's finding that cholangiocarcinoma has the highest OR2H1 expression rate (59%) among the three tumor types studied.
verification_notes: Live GDC API pull (api.gdc.cancer.gov/cnvs), gene_id=ENSG00000204688, cnv_change=Gain, de-duplicated by unique case_id (see or51e2.json's general CNV record for the same methodology note). CNV gain does not itself establish the protein-expression finding from Martin et al. -- this is a cross-check/corroboration signal, not independent proof of the paper's IHC/qRT-PCR expression percentages.

citation: RCSB PDB full-text search, live query
claim: No solved PDB structure exists for OR2H1 (0 hits on a full-text search of the RCSB PDB), unlike OR51E2 (structure 8F76, cryo-EM, 3.1 A).
verification_notes: Live RCSB PDB search API query (search.rcsb.org/rcsbsearch/v2/query, full_text service, query='OR2H1') returned HTTP 204 (no hits). Recorded as a real negative result, not an omission -- relevant because it means any future structural claims about OR2H1 (e.g. AlphaFold-predicted models) would be computational predictions, not experimentally solved structures, unlike OR51E2.

claim: No ChEMBL target record exists for OR2H1 -- zero bioactivity/ligand-binding data available, unlike OR51E2 (target CHEMBL4523454, 144 activity records).
verification_notes: Live ChEMBL REST API query (target/search.json?q=OR2H1) returned total_count=0. Consistent with OR2H1's role in the literature being as a CAR-T surface-antigen target rather than a studied small-molecule-ligand GPCR -- no natural/synthetic ligand has been characterized for OR2H1 in the reviewed literature either.

citation: Moffitt Cancer Center, Office of Innovation, technology listing 20MA027, "Olfactory Receptor Targeting Chimeric Antigen Receptor Expressing T cell (CAR-T) for Solid Tumors."
claim: A tech-transfer/licensing listing (not a confirmed granted patent) describing an OR2H1-targeting CAR-T construct for solid tumors, apparently related to or building on the Duke Martin et al. 2022 work.
verification_notes: COULD NOT FULLY VERIFY: the Moffitt technology-listing page (moffitt.org) returned HTTP 403 when fetched directly, so I could not confirm a specific patent application number, filing date, or inventor list -- only that the listing exists per search-engine indexing/snippets. Do not treat this as a confirmed granted patent; flag for a follow-up direct USPTO/Google Patents search by application number if this needs to be demo-ready. This is weaker-confidence than the two confirmed OR51E2 Duke patents (US10588884B2, US12186294B2).
